channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse — India & Global intelligence dashboard.

item.title: PM Modi must call all-party meet to discuss impact of West Asia conflict: Pawar
item.description: Pawar demanded that the PM convene an all-party meeting under his own chairmanship

item.title: depthfirst Commits up to $5M in Credits to Help Open Source Software Find and Fix Zero Day Vulnerabilities - The National Law Review
item.description: depthfirst Commits up to $5M in Credits to Help Open Source Software Find and Fix Zero Day Vulnerabilities The National Law Review

item.title: India-Oman FTA on track for June 1 start even as deal with Chile hits hurdles, says Commerce Minister
item.description: The India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement was signed in December 2025

item.title: Infiltrator killed by Army near the LoC in Poonch
item.description: An Army spokesman said soldiers detected “suspicious movement around 300 metres inside own territory in the general area of Krishna Ghati sector along the LoC at around 4 p.m.”

item.title: Analysis: Iran war hangs over Trump's China trip — and his presidency
item.description: There is no quick fix to the economic and political damage from the war.

item.title: Former Army personnel accused of killing three people, dies in police encounter in Uttar Pradesh
item.description: Gurpreet Singh, the suspect, committed the first murder on May 10 while traveling between Prayagraj and Chandauli, second and third on May 11, one on a train, another in a hospital, respectively

item.title: US faces rising costs with Iran war driving energy prices, inflation higher
item.description: Consumer prices spike in April, driven by soaring energy costs, with petrol prices up nearly 30 percent since last year.

item.title: ECB Rate Hikes Increasingly Likely, Nagel Tells Handelsblatt
item.description: The probability that the European Central Bank will need to raise borrowing costs due to the Iran war is rising, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Qualcomm, Nvidia, AMD and Intel plunge up to 15% as hot US inflation data dents AI rally sentiment
item.description: The Nasdaq-100 fell 2.3% amid a tech stock sell-off, despite a 16% April surge. Inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions affected investor sentiment, particularly in chip stocks like Qualcomm and Intel. The Fed may consider a rate hike as inflation rises due to the Iran conflict.

item.title: Junk-Rated Firms Rush to Reprice Debt as Demand for Loans Surges
item.description: Junk-rated firms are rushing to reprice and refinance existing US-dollar debt, seizing on renewed investor appetite for riskier loans to slash borrowing costs.

item.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
item.description: The company named Open Doors Partners, Unicorns Exchange, Pachamama Capital, Lionheart Ventures, Hiive, Forge Global, Sydecar and Upmarket as companies that are not authorized to provide access to buy or sell its shares.

item.title: Education Ministry gives fresh push for implementation of PM-SHRI in Bengal, Kerala, and T.N. post elections
item.description: Launched in September 2022, this five-year initiative aims to transform over 14,500 schools into “exemplar institutions” that lead the way in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020; only a limited time is left for the scheme to complete its five-year duration, the Ministry stated in a letter issued to the States

item.title: Chinese claims of ‘on site support’ to Pakistan during Operation Sindoor ‘corroborates’ what was known earlier: MEA
item.description: “Nations must reflect on whether supporting terror infrastructure affects their reputation and standing,” warns MEA; engineer confirms role of Chinese personnel in last year’s India-Pakistan clash

item.title: The case for owning cybersecurity stocks in the age of AI just got stronger
item.description: New evidence showed that hackers are using AI to accelerate more sophisticated cyberattacks.

item.title: Japanese Proverb of the Day: ‘Little fish grinding their teeth’; meaning, business lesson and why it still matters today
item.description: The proverb illustrates the futility of misdirected energy, symbolised by little fish grinding their teeth against unchangeable power. It encourages redirecting frustration towards actionable goals instead of exhausting oneself in futile anger.

item.title: Yogi Adityanath cuts UP ministers' convoys by 50%, urges public to save fuel after Modi's appeal amid West Asia crisis
item.description: Yogi Adityanath, the Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh proposed staggered office hours, encouraged public transport use, and called for reduced fuel consumption, emphasising austerity measures amid global instability as West Asia war continues.

item.title: Boeing Bets Comeback on Trump, China and an Elusive New Plane
item.description: When Richard Nixon landed in Beijing in 1972 to end a quarter-century of Chinese diplomatic and economic isolation, his hosts were so enthralled with the US president’s airliner that within months they ordered 10 Boeing Co. 707 jets. That purchase kicked off one of the most consequential commercial relationships in the planemaker’s 110-year history and helped turn China into the world’s second-largest aviation market

item.title: Four cheetah cubs die at Kuno in suspected leopard attack
item.description: The deaths in Madhya Pradesh hold significance as the four cubs were the first to be born in the open forest since India’s reintroduction programme for the big cat species — Project Cheetah — was launched in 2022; this is the largest number of cheetah casualties at Kuno in one instance, which witnessed the birth of several cubs in recent months.

item.title: Washington National Guard Aviation Crews Train for Wildfire Season - U.S. Department of War (.gov)
item.description: Washington National Guard Aviation Crews Train for Wildfire Season U.S. Department of War (.gov)

item.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
item.description: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages. Following a recent surge, maize planting is forecasted to decline, as farmers shift towards crops that are less fertilizer-dependent and lower in risk amid unpredictable weather conditions.

item.title: All India strike by MGNREGA workers on May 15
item.description: NREGA Sangharsh Morcha and All India Kisan Sabha have called for the strike against the government’s decision to discontinue the scheme.

item.title: Global turmoil is India’s 'wake-up call' to cut import dependence: Piyush Goyal
item.description: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urges domestic industry to embrace self-reliance amid global challenges. He calls for increased productivity and better use of trade agreements. The minister emphasizes reducing import dependence to bolster the economy. He also highlights the potential of electric vehicles and advanced technologies like AI for growth. This presents a significant opportunity for India to emerge s

item.title: IMD unveils weather model to provide ‘block level’ forecast of monsoon journey
item.description: It has been a long-standing aim of the IMD to provide hyper local forecasts to enable farmers to time their sowing precisely

item.title: Mistral AI SDK, TanStack Router hit in npm software supply chain attack - csoonline.com
item.description: Mistral AI SDK, TanStack Router hit in npm software supply chain attack csoonline.com

item.title: UN Chief Guterres Urges Urgent Climate Financing at Africa Forward Summit - ENA English
item.description: UN Chief Guterres Urges Urgent Climate Financing at Africa Forward Summit ENA English

item.title: Sam Altman rejects Elon Musk’s ‘stealing a charity’ claim in explosive OpenAI trial
item.description: Musk sued OpenAI and its leadership in August 2024, alleging that Altman and OpenAI President Greg Brockman convinced him to contribute $38 million to a nonprofit AI initiative meant to benefit humanity, only for the organisation to later pursue a profit-driven model.

item.title: BREAKING: Iran’s IRGC Launches Massive War Drill Amid Fragile Truce And Rising Tensions - WION
item.description: BREAKING: Iran’s IRGC Launches Massive War Drill Amid Fragile Truce And Rising Tensions WION

item.title: Attackers Use Fake OpenAI Model to Push Credential-Stealing Malware - Security Boulevard
item.description: Attackers Use Fake OpenAI Model to Push Credential-Stealing Malware Security Boulevard

item.title: Indian woman says picking fallen mangoes in Singapore may cost ₹3.5 lakh: ‘My India is the best’
item.description: The issue gained traction online as several users compared the regulation with practices in India, where people often freely access fruits from roadside trees without restrictions.

item.title: Fake Invitation Phishing Is Becoming a Remote Access Problem for CISOs - cyberpress.org
item.description: Fake Invitation Phishing Is Becoming a Remote Access Problem for CISOs cyberpress.org

item.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed governor, clears way for chair vote
item.description: The upper chamber voted to approve Warsh's nomination by a 51-45 vote, on a mostly party-line basis.

item.title: 'Failed Country, Asking For Help': Trump Announces Talks With Cuba
item.description: On May 1, Trump announced new economic sanctions targeting key sections of the Cuban economy.

item.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
item.description: Škoda Auto, a wholly owned subsidiary of the Volkswagen Group, has disclosed a data breach after attackers hacked its online shop and stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers. [...]

item.title: Dali ship operator charged over deadly Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: Six construction workers were killed and several vehicles plunged into the river after the cargo ship crashed into the bridge.

item.title: Russia Sees 2026 Oil Output Flat as Kyiv Steps Up Drone Strikes
item.description: Russia expects its oil production to remain flat in 2026 and grow modestly in the coming two years as its energy infrastructure comes under intense drone attacks from Ukraine.

item.title: India criticises China, raises 'reputation' question over support to Pakistan
item.description: “It is for nations… to reflect whether supporting attempts to protect terrorist infrastructure affects their reputation and standing,” says MEA spokesperson.

item.title: Android 17 to expand banking scam call and privacy protections
item.description: Android 17, expected to roll out next month, will introduce several security and privacy features focused on device theft, threat detection, and banking scam calls. [...]

item.title: Trump to meet with Xi for U.S.-China summit with Iran war, trade and Taiwan on the agenda - Spectrum News
item.description: Trump to meet with Xi for U.S.-China summit with Iran war, trade and Taiwan on the agenda Spectrum News

item.title: Google launches new Android security feature to help uncover spyware attacks
item.description: Intrusion Logging is a new part of Android’s Advanced Protection Mode, which aims to help protect human rights activists, journalists, and dissidents from government spyware attack and law enforcement forensic devices.

item.title: Anthropic announces new Claude-powered legal software as SaaS stocks continue to struggle - Yahoo Finance
item.description: Anthropic announces new Claude-powered legal software as SaaS stocks continue to struggle Yahoo Finance
